paper_title: Pollen analysis reveals plants foraged by Africanized honeybees in the southern Pantanal, Brazil.

methods.description: This method involves recording flowering species and observing honeybee foraging behavior in a natural forested patch to understand the pollen diet of Africanized honeybees.

steps.description: The flowering species present at the edge of the studied forested patch were recorded monthly in four plots of 5 x 20 m (100 m²).
steps.searchable_summary: record flowering species monthly in designated plots

steps.description: Direct observations on the honeybees foraging for pollen in flowers were monthly made in the studied plots.
steps.searchable_summary: observe and record honeybee foraging behavior monthly

methods.description: This method describes the installation of a pollen trap to collect pollen samples from a wild colony of A. mellifera to include species not registered in the field observations.

steps.description: A pollen trap was installed at the entrance of a wild colony of A. mellifera in a tree hole inside the forested patch.
steps.searchable_summary: install pollen trap at wild bee colony entrance

steps.description: The pollen trap was used for one day every month, except in the rainy period, when it remained on the hive for three months.
steps.searchable_summary: collect pollen samples monthly, extended during rainy season

methods.description: This method involves the preparation and analysis of pollen samples to identify pollen types and calculate frequency classes.

steps.description: Samples of pollen loads were kept in glycerin 50% and later analyzed with the classic European method.
steps.searchable_summary: preserve pollen samples in glycerin and prepare for analysis

steps.description: Pollen was mixed in 10 ml of 70% ethanol, centrifuged, and the sediment was washed in distilled water, left for 30 min in 1:1 glycerol:H2O, and centrifuged again.
steps.searchable_summary: process pollen samples with ethanol and centrifuge

steps.description: For light microscopy observations, slides were prepared using glycerin-jelly and sealed with paraffin.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare slides for microscopy with glycerin-jelly

steps.description: The identification of pollen types was based on the floristic survey and on pollen catalogues.
steps.searchable_summary: identify pollen types using floristic survey and catalogues

steps.description: For calculation of frequency classes of each pollen type a total of 300 pollen grains were counted per sample.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate frequency classes by counting pollen grains
